responseSet.title: Approaching the dream as a question: A self-analytical exploration on the expressive functions of dreams
responseSet.abstract:
[[RunningHead]]

Approaching the dream as a question: A self-analytical exploration on the expressive functions of dreams

Luiz Meyer

Luiz Meyer

São Paulo
Brazil

Luiz Meyer   CONTACT Luiz Meyer luimeyer@uol.com.br Rua Santa Cristina, 217 – CEP. 01443-020, Brazil
Dream, self analysis, infantile, question, emotional experience, confrontation

The author suggests that dreams are an expressive means through which the psychic apparatus delineates, construes and communicates an issue it is faced with. It is shown that there are substantial differences between this approach and more classical understandings of the nature and function of dreaming. Instead of laying emphasis on dream work as a defence system or as outlets of unconscious drives, the author highlights two further aspects of dreaming: (1) that in and through dreaming the psychic apparatus has developed a specific capacity to identify and express questions, problems and emotional experiences with which it is confronted, in a complex spiral and vertiginous way, as described in the paper; (2) that dreams encapsulate kernels of experience into “engrams” by constantly searching for ways of addressing and readdressing emotional experience.

Aborder le rêve comme une question : Une exploration auto-analytique des fonctions expressives des rêves.L'auteur de cet article suggère que les rêves constituent un moyen expressif par le biais duquel l'appareil psychique définit, construit et communique une question qu'il se doit d'affronter. L'auteur fait ressortir les différences substantielles entre ce mode d'approche et les conceptions plus classiques de la nature et fonction de l'activité onirique. Au lieu de mettre l'accent sur le travail du rêve en tant que système défensif ou bien comme fabrique de rejetons de pulsions inconscientes, l'auteur souligne deux autres aspects de l'activité onirique: (1) Dans et au travers de l'activité onirique, l'appareil psychique a développé une capacité spécifique d'identifier et d'exprimer les questions, problèmes et expériences émotionnelles auxquels il se trouve confronté, et ce, selon des modalités en spirale aussi complexes que vertigineuses, comme en témoignent les descriptions de l'auteur; (2) Les rêves encapsulent des noyaux d'expérience dans des « engrammes », en recherchant constamment les moyens de traiter et de retraiter les expériences émotionnelles.

Betrachtung des Traums als Frage: Eine selbstanalytische Erforschung der Ausdrucksfunktionen von Träumen.Für den Autor sind Träume ein Ausdrucksmittel zur Darstellung, Auslegung und Kommunikation einer Frage, der sich der psychische Apparat gegenübersieht. Es wird aufgezeigt, dass es wesentliche Unterschiede zwischen diesem Ansatz und traditionelleren Auffassungen vom Wesen und der Funktion des Träumens gibt. Statt die Rolle der Traumarbeit als Abwehrsystem oder Ventil für unbewusste Triebe zu betonen, hebt der Autor zwei weitere Aspekte des Träumens hervor: (1) Dass der psychische Apparat im Träumen und durch das Träumen eine spezielle Fähigkeit entwickelt hat, Fragen, Probleme und emotionales Erleben, womit er sich konfrontiert sieht, in komplexen Windungen und auf schwindelerregende Weise zu erkennen und auszudrücken, wie dies in dem Beitrag geschildert wird; (2) dass Träume Erlebniskerne in “Engrammen” zusammenfassen, indem sie ständig nach Wegen suchen, mit dem emotionalen Erleben umzugehen und sich erneut damit auseinanderzusetzen.

Accostarsi al sogno come a una domanda. Un'esplorazione autoanalitica sulle funzioni espressive dei sogni.L'autore suggerisce che i sogni sono dei mezzi di espressione attraverso cui l'apparato psichico delinea, costruisce e comunica una realtà con la quale si trova in quel momento alle prese. Si mostra qui come ci siano importanti differenze tra questo approccio e il modo più classico di intendere la natura e le funzioni del sogno. Anziché soffermarsi sulla natura difensiva del lavoro del sogno, o sul suo rappresentare il prodotto di pulsioni inconsce, l'autore evidenzia due aspetti ulteriori: (1) che con il sogno e attraverso il sogno l'apparato psichico ha sviluppato una capacità specifica di individuare ed esprimere le questioni, i problemi e le esperienze emotive con cui di volta in volta si confronta, in un processo vertiginoso e spiraliforme la cui complessità è pure descritta nell'articolo; (2) che i sogni racchiudono dei noccioli di esperienza all'interno di “engrammi” cercando costantemente dei modi per affrontare e riaffrontare le esperienze emotive.

Abordaje del sueño como cuestión: una exploración autoanalítica sobre su función expresiva.El autor sugiere que el sueño es un medio expresivo a través del cual el aparato psíquico delinea, construye y comunica una cuestión a la que se enfrenta. Se muestra que existen diferencias sustantivas entre este enfoque y las formas más clásicas de entender la naturaleza y la función del soñar. En vez de poner énfasis en el trabajo del sueño como sistema de defensa o como válvulas de escape de pulsiones inconscientes, el autor destaca dos aspectos adicionales del soñar: (1) que en el sueño y a través de él, el aparato psíquico desarrolla la capacidad específica de identificar y expresar cuestiones, problemas y experiencias emocionales a los que enfrenta, en una espiral compleja y vertiginosa como la que se describe en el artículo; (2) que los sueños encapsulan núcleos de experiencia en “engramas” mediante la búsqueda constante de maneras de abordar y volver a enfrentar la experiencia emocional.

responseSet.abstract:
[[RunningHead]]

Trauma and the destructive-transformative struggle: clinical perspectives

edited by Terrence McBride and Maureen Murphy, London and New York, Routledge, 2019, 236 pp., £29.99, ISBN: 978-0367333904

Marianne Leuzinger-Bohleber

Marianne Leuzinger-Bohleber

Marianne Leuzinger-Bohleber   m.leuzinger-bohleber@gmx.de

The book, which is dedicated to a central topic of today's psychoanalysis, is based on a 2014 conference, “Trauma, Destruction and Transformative Potential”. This took place in New York City over the weekend when the 9/11 Memorial Museum opened there. As Maureen Murphy points out in her brief introduction, this made the theme of the conference very topical.

As is well known, psychoanalysis began as a theory of trauma, but Freud's radical turn to inner fantasies and conflicts influenced the psychoanalytic community for years. Although Freud himself remained interested in the effects of early traumatizations throughout his life, a kind of taboo arose in the psychoanalytic community for decades, putting the influence of external reality behind psychic determination by unconscious phantasies and inner mental states. This changed in recent decades, when a large number of psychoanalytic or interdisciplinary conferences and workshops on trauma took place and a flood of articles and books were published. The volume edited by McBride and Murphy offers an excellent overview of the current state of knowledge in psychoanalytic trauma research and the still virulent controversies. It also provides differentiated insights into treatment opportunities and challenges as well as specific technical requirements.

The volume is clearly divided into four parts with 16 chapters and lays out its focus on “Clinical Perspectives”. Almost all contributions contain more or less detailed case studies, mainly from psychoanalyses with adults, but also from children and adolescents. The question of the relationship between trauma and inner psychic conflicts runs like a thread through the theoretical framing:

The dichotomy is between those more traditional analysts who believe that preexisting intrapsychic conflict is reflected in the individuaĺs response to trauma as well as on the development of conflict subsequent to the trauma and those relational analysts and others who emphasize the role of external reality, especially relationships with important early objects, as the cause of trauma. In this latter view, internal mental structures are deemphasized as being significant causative factors. (McBride, 26)

[[RunningHead]]

At the risk of thinking: an intellectual biography of Julia Kristeva

by Alice Jardine, edited by Mari Ruti, New York, London, Oxford, New Delhi, Sydney, Bloomsbury Academic, 2020, 385 pp., (hardback), ISBN: 9781501341342, $26.95 (paperback), ISBN: 9781501341335

Rosemary Balsam

Rosemary Balsam

New Haven
USA

New Haven
USA

New Haven
USA

Rosemary Balsam   Rosemary.balsam@yale.edu

Julia Kristeva is prominent as a Bulgarian-French “cosmopolitan” (Jardine, 10) public intellectual and a brilliant polymath. Her fields of immersion cover a vast range: philosophy, anthropology, culture, history, linguistics, semiotics, literary criticism, sociology, political theory, gender, theology, social activism and – importantly for her interdisciplinary convictions and this review – psychoanalysis. She is, in her words, also a “contestatory” intellectual, and one who “has never stopped thinking … about how to live a thinking life” (Jardine, 2). Anglophones sometimes say that Kristeva’s theoretical writing is impenetrable – at least in English translation, and she has not been fully embraced by academicians in the United States by various critics on both the right and left. Yet, perceiving the passion of her creativity and importance of the topics, many become avidly intrigued and rewarded with her challenge. Academic scholars who have paid close attention to her thinking about thinking and her writing over the last five decades have themselves advanced the history of ideas in the West, such as in dealing with the complexities of feminism.

Kristeva’s compelling personality and comportment as a woman, moreover, evoke such adjectives from the press as “glamorous”, “almond-eyed” and “beautiful”. Some have criticized her lifestyle as elite, due to her taste in clothes and fondness for her large family house in the Île de Ré. Upsettingly, though, and shockingly to Kristeva, in 2018 she was accused of being a secret agent for the Communist regime in Bulgaria, allegedly having been recruited in 1971, five years after she had moved to Paris. She, others and Alice Jardine her biographer vigorously deny this. Copies of the letters she had taken to the embassy to mail to her parents appeared in this 400 page “Agent Sabina” file released publicly, and had been therefore intercepted. Her oeuvre and her career in the West have certainly been founded on the antithesis of such activity. Critical reactions to both Kristeva herself and her work seem to intertwine, and 629

responseSet.title: Establishing a Therapeutic Relationship: An Initial Stake in Psychotherapy with Young Psychotic Adults
responseSet.authorMast: Vincent di Rocco and  Raphaël Minjard
responseSet.abstract:
[[RunningHead]]

Establishing a Therapeutic Relationship: An Initial Stake in Psychotherapy with Young Psychotic Adults

Vincent di Rocco and Raphaël Minjard

Raphaël Minjard

Vincent.di-rocco@orange.fr
5, Avenue Pierre Mendès-France, 69676, Bron, France
http://orcid.org/0000-0003-4285-3346

Bron, France

Raphaël Minjard  

Psychoanalysis; psychosis; psychotherapy; symbolisation; desymbolisation

Although psychosis and psychoanalysis have been closely linked since the earliest Freudian theories, the psychoanalytic treatment of psychotic states still presents major difficulties. The establishment of a therapeutic situation allowing the deployment of psychic work is still uncertain. The resumption of the work of S. Freud and DW. Winnicott associated with the development of the theorization of a metapsychology of the process of symbolization by the French school (Green, Roussillon) allows a revival of the reflection on the stakes of the establishment of the therapeutic situation. This theorization of representative processes makes it possible to consider the psychotherapeutic approach to psychoses from the angle of a “psychopathology of the psychic apparatus” and thus to model the psychic dynamics involved in psychotic problems which initially appear in a chaotic form. The apparent failure of the psychic processes, in their linking and transforming activity with which the psychotic states are confronted, can then be thought of as the seat of a particular psychic work that can provide essential support to the therapeutic work. Two cases studies illustrate these processes.

responseSet.title: Terttu Eskelinen
responseSet.abstract:
[[RunningHead]]

Terttu Eskelinen

Guillermo Bodner

Guillermo Bodner

Barcelona, Spain
gbodnerp@gmail.com

Guillermo Bodner  

On 12 February, Terttu Eskelinen (Pielavesi, 1931 - Barcelona, 2021), a beloved teacher, friend and colleague, passed away. She had arrived in Barcelona after living in several European countries following her psychoanalytic training.

Terttu Eskelinen was born in Pielavesi, a small town in northern Finland, which she always remembered fondly, especially the snowy forest landscapes where she spent her childhood. She attended school in Finland, where she learned Swedish and other languages due to the fluctuating frontiers of the time.

At a young age, Terttu Eskelinen left Finland, her native country, to move to Switzerland to continue her psychological and psychoanalytic training. There she met and worked with Raymond de Saussure and Jean Piaget. During her stay in Switzerland, she met the Catalan psychoanalyst Pere Folch, who would later become her husband. She had her first analytic training at the Swiss Society of Psychoanalysis, which she completed at the British Psychoanalytical Society, where she was educated by such teachers as Anna Freud, Hanna Segal, Ester Bick, Betty Joseph, Herbert Rosenfeld, Donald Meltzer and others. This experience was fundamental to her as a psychoanalyst and as a person. It was the time of consolidation of Kleinian ideas in Great Britain and the beginning of their wide influence in other European countries.

Once settled in Catalonia, she soon experienced the country, its language and culture, and the fight for freedom and democracy in times of the Franco dictatorship. In addition to all the restrictions of a dictatorial regime, the use of the Catalan language was outlawed. Terttu Eskelinen was a woman of firm convictions and admirable vitality, with great love for her husband Pere Folch, for her daughters Laura and Helka, and for her grandchildren. Her home, always open to friends, was a pleasant and welcoming place where friends would enjoy the generosity, friendship, conversations and humour of Terttu and Pere.

Terttu’s broad vision of psychoanalysis stimulated her interest in all cultural manifestations, in which both she and her husband Pere Folch were very interested. Both were enthusiasts of music, painting and literature. It was common to hear Terttu recalling passages from Shakespeare’s plays, which she knew in detail, or from Rilke’s poetry, which she associated to situations experienced in her clinical material. Her psychoanalytic ear was especially tuned to listen to the manifestations of the unconscious in art.

responseSet.title: A case of trichotillomania and trichophagy: Fantasies of cannibalism
responseSet.abstract:
[[RunningHead]]

A case of trichotillomania and trichophagy: Fantasies of cannibalism

Britt-Marie Schiller

Britt-Marie Schiller

Saint Louis
MO
USA

Britt-Marie Schiller   CONTACT Britt-Marie Schiller schillbm@webster.edu 7700 Clayton Road, Suite 204, Saint Louis, MO 63117, USA
Trichotillomania, trichophagy, cannibalism, mother–infant bond, object relations

Cannibalistic fantasies underlie some core mechanisms in psychoanalysis: identification, incorporation, introjection, and internalization. Freud (1917) saw melancholia as an incorporation of the object by devouring it, and Julia Kristeva (1989) takes the melancholic cannibalistic imagination to be a repudiation of loss, a destruction in order to possess the object. The author argues that cannibalism is a useful concept for understanding the double dynamics of trichotillomania and trichophagy - as destructive disconnection followed by incorporation of the hair; both suggest a magical reconnection with what was disconnected in order to establish a bond with the mother/analyst. The paradoxical passion of consuming the object of desire, symbolized here by the hair (resembling an umbilical cord and connection to the maternal object) captures the cannibalistic fantasy of never losing the object by destroying it.

Les fantasmes de cannibalisme sous-tendent certains des mécanismes clés en psychanalyse: identification, incorporation, introjection et internalisation. Freud (1917) considérait la mélancolie comme une incorporation de l'objet par dévoration; Julia Kristeva (1989), quant à elle, envisage l'imaginaire mélancolique du cannibalisme comme étant un désaveu de la perte, une destruction qui vise à posséder l'objet. L'auteure de cet article soutient que le cannibalisme est un concept permettant de comprendre la double dynamique de la trichotillomanie et de la trichophagie, en tant qu'une déconnexion destructrice suivie de l'incorporation de l'objet; l'une et l'autre tendent vers une reconnexion magique avec ce qui avait été déconnecté afin d'établir un lien avec la mère/analyste. La passion paradoxale de consommation de l'objet du désir, symbolisé ici par les cheveux (apparentés à un cordon ombilical et un lien à l'objet maternel) est au cœur du fantasme de cannibalisme, celui de ne jamais perdre l'objet en le détruisant.

Kannibalistische Fantasien liegen einigen Kernmechanismen der Psychoanalyse zugrunde: Identifikation, Inkorporation, Introjektion und Internalisierung. Für Freud (1917) war die Melancholie die Einverleibung des Objekts durch Verschlingen, und Julia Kristeva (1989) versteht die melancholische kannibalistische Fantasie als eine Zurückweisung von Verlust, eine Vernichtung, um das Objekt zu besitzen. Die Autorin argumentiert, dass Kannibalismus ein zweckdienliches Konzept zum Verständnis der doppelten Dynamik von Trichotillomanie und Trichophagie sei – eine destruktive Trennung, der eine Einverleibung des Haars folgt. Beides suggeriert eine magische Wiederverbindung mit dem, was vorher abgetrennt war, um eine Bindung mit der Mutter/dem Analytiker bzw. der Analytikerin herzustellen. Die paradoxe Leidenschaft, das Objekt der Begierde – hier durch das Haar symbolisiert (welches einer Nabelschnur und Verbindung zum mütterlichen Objekt ähnelt) zu verspeisen, erfasst die kannibalistische Fantasie, ein Objekt durch Vernichten niemals zu verlieren.

Alcuni dei meccanismi psichici fondamentali individuati dalla psicoanalisi sono sottesi da fantasie cannibaliche: tra questi l'identificazione, l'incorporazione, l'introiezione e l'internalizzazione. Freud (1917) vedeva nella melanconia un modo di incorporare l'oggetto divorandolo, mentre per Julia Kristeva (1989) l'immaginario cannibalico caratteristico della melanconia è una strategia per ripudiare la perdita: una distruzione il cui fine sarebbe quello di possedere l'oggetto. L'autore sostiene che il cannibalismo è un concetto utile per comprendere la doppia dinamica della tricotillomania e della tricofagia - da lui interpretate come disconnessione distruttiva seguita dall'incorporazione dei capelli. Entrambi gli atti evocano una riconnessione magica con ciò che è stato scollegato in modo da stabilire un legame con la madre/analista. La passione paradossale che porta a consumare l'oggetto del desiderio, simbolizzato qui dai capelli (che rinviano a un cordone ombelicale e alla connessione all'oggetto materno) costituisce dunque la rappresentazione della fantasia cannibalica di distruggere l'oggetto per non doverlo mai perdere.

Subyacen fantasías de caníbalismo a algunos de los mecanismos centrales del psicoanálisis: la identificación, la incorporación, la introyección y la internalización. Freud (1917) consideró la melancolía como una incorporación del objeto mediante su devoramiento, y Julia Kristeva (1989) entiende la fantasía caníbal melancólica como un repudio de la pérdida, una destrucción a fin de poseer el objeto. La autora sostiene que el canibalismo es un concepto útil para comprender la dinámica doble de la tricotilomanía y la tricofagia (como desconexión destructiva seguida de la incorporación del cabello); ambas sugieren una reconexión mágica con lo que estaba desconectado, a fin de establecer un vínculo con la madre/analista. La paradójica pasión de consumir el objeto de deseo, simbolizada aquí por el cabello (parecido al cordón umbilical y conexión con el objeto materno), plasma la fantasía caníbal de no perder nunca el objeto por medio de su destrucción.

responseSet.abstract:
[[RunningHead]]

La práctica del Psicoanálisis. Evolución y actualidad

by Juan Pablo Jiménez and Josefina Figueroa cox, Providencia, Santiago, Chile, Avendia Andrés Bello 1587-1591, 2019, 231 pp., $19.80, ISBN: 978-956-220-418-7

Susana Fischbein

Susana Fischbein

Susana Fischbein   susyvinfisch@gmail.com

This book is the most valuable product of a collective effort by Chilean psychoanalysts who have been participating in an experience that began more than 30 years ago at the Salvador Corporation in Santiago, Chile – an institution founded and continually inspired by Juan Pablo Jiménez. The institution was created as part of a training programme for student practitioners of psychology and psychiatry residents at a university hospital in Santiago, which later continued as a postgraduate training programme at the University of Chile's Psychiatry Department.

One of the book's multiple virtues is its break from classical, well-established methods of clinical practice and its openness to modalities that best respond to current conflicts and the most frequent types of present-day consultation. Drawing on teaching experience closely tied to clinical practice, the authors of each chapter examine the relation between theory and practice from different epistemological perspectives, including the traditional conceptual contributions of Freud, Klein, Ferenczi, Bion, Winnicott and Sandler, among others, but also calling upon the more recent perspectives of psychodynamic, intersubjective and interactionist researchers, psychoanalysts and psychotherapists, mostly influenced by British and American psychoanalytic literature. These professionals follow the criteria established by evidence-based practice. As Jiménez puts it, the book condenses “everything we have done and thought over all these years” (15).

Each chapter reflects the position of the different groups of authors and their approach to the issues in which they specialize. In Chapter 1, “Psychoanalysis Behind the Scenes: Psychotherapeutic Practice Modified by Experience”, Figueroa and Seeger revisit the path that led them to create the group. They were responding to the need for specialized programmes for therapists focused on “training in and management of focal techniques and ‘brief interventions’”. Their purpose was to modify the modalities of care which – imbued with the teaching of long-term therapies – did not match either the interests of therapists in training or the needs of those consulting. Their aim was to organize a formal postgraduate programme in psychoanalytic focal psychotherapy. They moved out of the hospital for political reasons and founded the Salvador Corporation, whose instructors were, at first, psychoanalysts from the Chilean Psychoanalytic Association (APCH-IPA). As the psychoanalytic psychotherapists trained at the institution advanced to remarkable levels, they were included as instructors and were granted full member standing in the Corporation.

responseSet.title: Gender and transgender: a metapsychological contribution to the genesis of the sexual ego
responseSet.abstract:
[[RunningHead]]

Gender and transgender: a metapsychological contribution to the genesis of the sexual ego**

Susann Heenen-Wolff

Susann Heenen-Wolff

Belgium

Bruxelles
Belgium

Susann Heenen-Wolff   CONTACT Susann Heenen-Wolff susann.wolff@uclouvain.be Rue du Relais 105, 1050 Bruxelles
Transsexuality/transgender – gender/anatomy – construction of the ego – the “sexual” – primary/secondary identifications

Transsexuality or transgender has become an increasingly visible and widespread phenomenon in recent years. From a psychoanalytic perspective, it has so far not been possible to understand the lack of identification with one's own anatomical body. The author distinguishes between the formation of an early body ego as a result of primary identification and that of a sexual ego as part of secondary identifications. Identical and trans-identical orientation could arise against the background of how, when gender difference is discovered, the primary identification is processed, ‘translated', in an ‘après-coup’ movement.

Les phénomènes transsexuels ou transgenres sont devenus de plus en plus visibles et répandus ces dernières années. La perspective psychanalytique n'a pas permis jusqu'à présent d'éclairer la compréhension du manque d'identification de l'individu à son propre corps anatomique. L'auteure de cet article établit une distinction entre la formation d'un moi corporel précoce, issu de l'identification primaire, et celle d'un moi sexuel, produit en partie par les identifications secondaires. L'orientation identique ou trans-identique prendrait sa source dans la façon dont l'identification primaire est intégrée et « traduite » après-coup, au moment de la découverte de la différence des sexes.

Transsexualität bzw. Transidentität ist in den letzten Jahren ein zunehmend sichtbares und sich verbreitendes Phänomen geworden. Aus psychoanalytischer Perspektive ist bisher nicht gelungen, das Ausbleiben von Identifizierung mit dem eigenen anatomischen Körper nachzuvollziehen. Die Autorin unterscheidet zwischen der Ausbildung eines frühen Körper-Ichs in Folge primärer Identifizierung und der eines geschlechtlichen Ichs im Rahmen sekundärer Identifizierungen. Idente und transidente Orientierung könnte entstehen vor dem Hintergrund, wie mit dem Entdecken des Geschlechtsunterschiedes die primäre Identifizierung in einer nachträglichen Bewegung verarbeitet, „übersetzt“ wird.

Negli ultimi anni i fenomeni della transessualità e dell'identità transgenere sono divenuti sempre più visibili e diffusi. Finora non è stato possibile comprendere la mancanza di identificazione con il proprio corpo anatomico da una prospettiva psicoanalitica. L'autrice distingue tra la formazione di un io corporeo precoce, frutto dell'identificazione promaria, e quella di un io sessuale che farebbe invece parte del quadro delle identificazioni secondarie. Alla base dell'orientamento identico o trans-identico potrebbero esserci una serie di fattori: non solo il modo e il momento in cui è stata scoperta la differenza sessuale, ma anche il modo in cui l'identificazione primaria viene elaborata e 'tradotta' in un movimento di 'après-coup''.

En los últimos años, la transexualidad o transgénero se ha convertido en un fenómeno cada vez más visible y extendido. Aún no ha sido posible comprender, desde una perspectiva psicoanalítica, la falta de identificación con el propio cuerpo anatómico. La autora distingue entre la formación de un yo corporal temprano como resultado de la identificación primaria y la formación de un yo sexual en el marco de las identificaciones secundarias. La orientación idéntica y transidéntica podría surgir en la manera en que se procesa la identificación primaria en el contexto del descubrimiento de la diferencia entre los sexos, “traducida” en un movimiento après-coup.

responseSet.title: Summary page issue 1, 2021
responseSet.abstract:
[[RunningHead]]

Summary page issue 1, 2021

In her Editorial, Dana Birksted-Breen reflects on the issue of temporality at the heart of psychoanalysis, and how time is transformed during the Covid-19 pandemic. The new experience of remote sessions has created the possibility of ‘unpicking’ the threads of the usual setting, enabling authors to develop ideas about what is normally left silent.

“What it is like to read Freud again in the time of the coronavirus pandemic?” asks Jonathan Lear. In his close reading of Freud's “On transience,” Lear’s paper brings to light how Freud’s essay might be read differently with the thoughts of world-catastrophe on our minds.

Rosine Perelberg’s paper describes the analysis of a patient during the first six months of the pandemic when the setting was profoundly disrupted. In the absence of the physicality of the setting, the most primitive anxieties about the symbiotic relationship with the mother were expressed and contained in the transference and countertransference.

Joona Taipale’s considers the overlooked issue of interpersonal understanding from a Winnicottian perspective. Focusing on one of Winnicott’s letters to Klein, the paper explores the successes and shortcomings of interpersonal encounters – both in everyday life, in clinical settings, and in the historical community of researchers.

Originally, the term ‘enactment’ was used to describe an aspect of countertransference expressed by subtle actions within the analytical process, as opposed to the noisier performances of ‘acting-out’, explains Valeria Corbella. In her empirical and conceptual study, she examines the history, translations and uses of the term enactment, along with its epistemological status.

Giampaolo Sasso identifies a projective-introjective ‘oscillatory dynamic’ that forms the basis of the mother-infant relationship but also the foundation of psychoanalytic theory. Arguing that the ‘oscillatory dynamic’ is present on a neurological level in the maternal interaction with the infant, Sasso says the dynamic also helps explain the variety and complexity of psychoanalytic models, suggesting an important interface between neuroscience and psychoanalysis.

responseSet.title: Summary Page, Issue 4, 2021
responseSet.abstract:
[[RunningHead]]

Summary Page, Issue 4, 2021

Psychoanalytic Theory and Technique

Bion’s epistemological contributions drew on the work of the British philosopher of science, R. B. Braithwaite, more than has hitherto been acknowledged, argues Barnet Malin. Bion’s goal to bring an empirically based scientific model to psychoanalytic theory and practice was heavily influenced by Braithwaite’s “scientific deductive system” - the notion that empirical scientific methods can be applied to “unconscious mental processes.” Bion’s turn from the scientific deductive system towards his later developments can be understood in this context.

Examining the poor integration of soma and psyche in severely disturbed patients, Sebastian Leikert formulates the concept of “encapsulated body engrams” to refer to the defence structures revolving around the disorganization and inhibition of an affected bodily impulse. These engrams are highly repetitive and not susceptible to symbolic transformation. Leikert looks at how the engrams can be unblocked in analysis through body feelings which emerge in the chain of associations as attempts to communicate.

Child and Adolescent Psychoanalysis

Psychosis remains a challenging area to treat in psychoanalysis, writes Vincent di Rocco and Raphaël Minjard. Drawing on Green’s and Roussillon’s ideas about processes of symbolization, Di Rocco argues for the need to symbolize the difficulty of symbolization in the therapeutic work with psychotic adolescents. The work of construction of meaning and the recognition of desymbolisation are equally important for the establishment of the therapeutic relationship with these patients.

History of Psychoanalysis

Gonzalo Grau Perez's and Guillermo Milán's paper examines the reception of Lacanian ideas in Uruguayan psychoanalysis in the 1960s - at a time when Kleinian thought prevailed. Examining a variety of sources - theoretical papers, clinical cases, media publications, seminar transcripts and institutional documents - the authors look at the different controversies and crossroads that characterised the reception of Lacanian ideas in the Kleinian context.

responseSet.title: An incandescent self-exploration: Ambiguities and unconscious logic in Day of Wrath (Vredens Dag), directed by Carl Theodor Dreyer (Denmark 1943)
responseSet.abstract:
[[RunningHead]]

An incandescent self-exploration: Ambiguities and unconscious logic in Day of Wrath (Vredens Dag), directed by Carl Theodor Dreyer (Denmark 1943)

Cesare Secchi

Cesare Secchi

Reggio Emilia
Italy

Cesare Secchi   CONTACT Cesare Secchi cesare.secchi@virgilio.it Società Psicoanalitica Italiana, Via Terrachini 30, 42122 Reggio Emilia, Italy
Unconscious, symmetry principle, Matte Blanco, paranoid anxiety, mysticism, Dreyer

The author examines the paradoxical dramatic dynamics of the Dreyer's masterpiece, according the Matte Blanco's theoretical model, as a tormented path of self awareness that consists in a progressive immersion in the unconscious. Anne, the young wife of an old Lutheran pastor, marginally involved in the witchcraft trial of a friend of her mother (now dead, but suspected to be a sorceress as well), falls in love for his son-in-law, leaving the child-wife position held so far. It's a new very strong vital drive for Anne, to which she adds the belief to own the magic powers of the witches: the ability to evoke the living and the dead, kill with thought and communicate through dreams. All the emotions are at an infinite level of intensity. Nevertheless, after she's left by her lover and after her husband's wished-upon death, the protagonist confesses her presumed witchcraft before the husband's coffin and, in the derangement of ambiguities, affective contradictions and shift of position, she attains a sort of mystic epiphany (significantly represented by the flow of filmic images): maybe a contact with the deepest layer of the mind where, according to MatteBlanco, the symmetrization is total.

L'autore prende in esame la paradossale dinamica drammatica del capolavoro di Dreyer, ipotizzata come un sofferto percorso di conoscenza che consiste in una progressiva immersione nell'inconscio, secondo il modello di Matte Blanco. Anne, la protagonista, è la giovane moglie di un anziano pastore luterano, la quale, tangenzialmente coinvolta in un processo per stregoneria (la madre della stessa Anne, ora defunta, era stata sospettata di un analogo delitto), si innamora del figliastro, rifiutando il ruolo di moglie bambina tenuto finora. Si accende in lei una potente vitalità pulsionale, alla quale si viene ad aggiungere la convinzione di possedere i magici poteri delle streghe: evocare i vivi e i morti, uccidere col pensiero e comunicare attraverso i sogni. Le emozioni della protagonista raggiungono un livello infinito d'intensità. Peraltro, dopo la strana morte del marito da lei desiderata, nonché abbandonata dall'amante, Anne confessa la sua presunta stregoneria e, nella vertigine delle ambiguità, dei contrasti affettivi e dei ribaltamenti di fronte, approda a una sorta di epifania mistica (espressivamente rappresentata nel flusso delle immagini cinematografiche). Forse un contatto con lo strato più profondo della mente, dove, secondo Matte Blanco, la simmetrizzazione sarebbe totale.

responseSet.title: Summary Page, Issue 6, 2021
responseSet.abstract:
[[RunningHead]]

Summary Page, Issue 6, 2021

Psychoanalytic Theory and Technique

Bion spent his life as a scholar transposing his theory of groups into his theory of individual psychoanalysis, argues Giuseppe Civitarese. In Bion’s model of analytic field theory, the analyst sees in the analytical pair not two isolated subjects that interact, but a group. There is no “fact” of analysis that cannot be heard as unconsciously co-created. Highlighting the group inspiration of the late Bion helps us to grasp the meaning of this technical principle, so easily misunderstood, and vice versa.

Arguing in favour of Strachey’s translation of Freud’s nachträglichkeit as “deferred action,” Steven Groarke presents clinical observations on a patient’s emerging sense of the past; the paper discusses the continued importance of “deferred action” and the implications of “backwards causation.” Engaging with Winnicott’s theory of object-use to consider the problem of memory, Groarke introduces the concept of “reclamation” as a type of re-descriptive memory.

Richard Zimmer looks at aspects of the termination of psychoanalysis that are connected to the dissolution of symbiotic ties with the analyst. Relinquishment of the frame can provide an impetus for consolidation and reworking of an identification with the analyst, which may enable the patient to develop a personal way of thinking about the unconscious that differs from that of the analyst. Termination may provide an opportunity for self-analytic work beyond what could have been attained within the formal analytic setting.

Child and Adolescent Psychoanalysis

Infants can express emotional distress through gaze avoidance which can be very subtle, says Bjorn Salomonsson. Gaze avoidance elicits important theoretical questions: what does the child seem to avoid in the adult’s eyes, and how can we conceptualise the psychodynamics behind the symptom? Technical questions are also raised: how can the therapist make contact with an infant who avoids the eyes? How can the countertransference help the analyst understand the dyad’s emotional communication?

responseSet.title: Letter from Vancouver
responseSet.abstract:
[[RunningHead]]

Letter from Vancouver

Endre Koritar

Endre Koritar

Endre Koritar   elkoritar@gmail.com

The Vancouver 2021 IPA Congress will be a virtual event, as have most other conferences been recently, due to the dangerous COVID-19 pandemic. Ironically, this is the second time that a SARS virus has sidelined a Canadian city as host to the Congress. In 2003, it was scheduled for Toronto, but the SARS pandemic resulted in its cancellation. Now Vancouver has suffered the same fate due to SARS-CoV-2, the cause of COVID-19. Although I am sure that the Congress will be a success, it is unfortunate that Vancouver and the Canadian Society will once again not have a chance to indulge the international community with Canadian hospitality. The local Western Branch (WB) society was looking forward to showcasing Vancouver as one of the most naturally beautiful places in the world.

Situated at the end of the Fraser Valley, where the river meets the sea, and nestled between the Cascadian coastal mountains and Vancouver Island, it has a similar climate to another city on the 49th Parallel, Paris. The mild winters and pleasant summers result in a lifestyle focusing on outdoors activities: swimming at one of its many beaches in the summer, skiing the local mountains in the winter, sailing and golfing all year round, climbing the Grouse Grind, or playing beach volleyball, Vancouverites love being outdoors, and respecting the environment. Indeed, it has been considered one of the greenest cities in the world.

This respect for nature is also evident in the First Nations’ cultural heritage as Caretakers of the Earth, striving to live in harmony with nature. Vancouver is situated on the unceded native lands of the Musqueam, Squamish and Tsleil-Waututh Nations, and it is customary for all public events held on their territory to acknowledge their rights to the land that has become inhabited by a multicultural mosaic of people from all continents except Antarctica. This rich cultural mosaic results in several cultural festivals and celebrations throughout the year. Vancouverites, like most Canadians, enjoy celebrating and socializing. This is reflected in the active café and restaurant scenes in Yaletown, Gastown, English Bay, The Drive, the West End, Granville Street, etc.

responseSet.title: How does reading (dis)locate space? Reading “The Call of the Woods” (“La voix du bois”) by Pierre Bergounioux
responseSet.abstract:
[[RunningHead]]

How does reading (dis)locate space? Reading “The Call of the Woods” (“La voix du bois”) by Pierre Bergounioux

Frédéric Vinot and Sara Bédard-Goulet

Sara Bédard-Goulet

Nice
France

Tartu
Estonia

Sara Bédard-Goulet  

Metapsychology, Freudian theory, art, literature, representability/figurability

This multidisciplinary article shows how reading creates spaces, based on Pierre Bergounioux's short narrative ‘La voix du bois', where the narrator recalls his first reading as an ‘event' that dislocated his reality. First, a literary analysis of this text identifies three types of spatial configurations between fiction and reality: through ubiquity (Rosolato), contact points (Freud) and extimacy (Lacan). Second, multiple losses in the text are examined by a metapsychological analysis of reading based on a re-reading of Freud’s text on the Wunderblock (the periodical inexcitability of the perceptive system would not only contributes to form time representations but also space representations), and on a highlight of the drives at stake in reading, which rely on a quest of lost objects (gaze and voice). A third part of the article connects the three spatial configurations with three types of objects present in the act of reading: the read object necessitates a represented and stable geometrical space, the lost object holes representation and de-stablises space (Schilder), das Ding engages a beyond of representation, dislocating temporarily every representable space and causing an event.

Cet article pluridisciplinaire, qui est basé sur La voix du bois, un petit récit de Pierre Bergounioux, montre comment la lecture crée des espaces. Le narrateur de ce récit se remémore sa première lecture, un « événement » qui est venu désarticuler sa réalité. En premier lieu, une analyse littéraire de ce texte permet d'identifier trois types de configurations spatiales entre fiction et réalité : ubiquité (Rosolato), points de contact (Freud) et extimité (Lacan). Deuxièmement, la multiplicité des pertes au sein du texte est étudiée à travers une analyse métapsychologique de la lecture fondée sur une relecture du texte de Freud sur le Wunderblock (l'inexcitabilité périodique du système perceptif contribuerait non seulement à la formation de représentations temporelles mais également à celle de représentations spatiales), et une mise en exergue des pulsions à l'œuvre dans la lecture qui reposent sur la recherche d'objets perdus (regard et voix). Enfin, dans une troisième partie, l'article relie les trois configurations spatiales à trois types d'objets qui sont présent dans l'acte de lire : l'objet lu requiert un espace géométrique représenté et stable, l'objet perdu creuse un trou dans la représentation et dé-stabilise l'espace (Schilder), das Ding engage un au-delà de la représentation, désarticulant temporairement tout espace représentable et causant un événement.

Dieser multidisziplinäre Beitrag zeigt auf der Grundlage von Pierre Bergouniouxs kurzer Erzählung ‚La voix du bois‘, wie das Lesen Räume schafft. Der Erzähler erinnert sich an seine erste Leseerfahrung als ein ‚Ereignis‘, das seine Realität disloziert hat. Zunächst ermittelt eine literarische Analyse des Textes 3 Arten räumlicher Gestaltungen zwischen Fiktion und Realität: durch Ubiquität (Rosolato), Berührungspunkte (Freud) und Extimität (Lacan). Im zweiten Schritt werden vielfache Verluste im Text durch eine metapsychologische Analyse des Lesens anhand einer erneuten Lektüre von Freuds Notiz über den Wunderblock (die periodisch auftretende Nichterregbarkeit des Wahrnehmungssystems trüge nicht nur zur Bildung zeitlicher, sondern auch räumlicher Repräsentationen bei) und anhand der Hervorhebung jener Triebe untersucht, die beim Lesen auf dem Spiel stehen und die auf eine Suche nach verlorenen Objekten (Blick und Stimme) angewiesen sind. In einem dritten Teil dieses Beitrags werden die drei räumlichen Gestaltungen mit drei Arten von Objekten in Verbindung gebracht, die beim Leseakt gegenwärtig sind: das gelesene Objekt benötigt einen repräsentierten und beständigen geometrischen Raum; das verlorene Objekt durchlöchert die Repräsentation und destabilisiert den Raum (Schilder), das Ding belegt etwas außerhalb der Repräsentation, disloziert zeitweise jeglichen darstellbaren Raum und löst ein Ereignis aus.

Questo articolo multidisciplinare mostra come leggere crei degli spazi prendendo spunto dal racconto 'La voce del bosco' di Pierre Bergounioux, in cui il narratore ricorda la prima esperienza di lettura come un 'evento' che ha dislocato la sua realtà. Inizieremo dunque con l'individuare, attraverso l'analisi letteraria del testo, tre tipi di configurazioni spaziali tra finzione e realtà, caratterizzate rispettivamente da ubiquità (Rosolato), punti di contatto (Freud) ed estimità (Lacan). Procederemo quindi a esaminare le numerose perdite contenute nel testo attraverso un'analisi metapsicologica della lettura basata su una rilettura del testo freudiano sul Notes magico (la periodica non suscettibilità del sistema percettivo contribuirebbe a formare non solo rappresentazioni del tempo, ma anche dello spazio) e portando inoltre l'attenzione sulle pulsioni coinvolte nell'atto del leggere, rivolte alla ricerca di oggetti perduti (lo sguardo e la voce). La terza parte dell'articolo collega le tre configurazioni spaziali di cui si è detto con tre tipi di oggetti presenti nell'atto del leggere: l'oggetto che viene letto necessita di uno spazio geometrico rappresentato e stabile, l'oggetto perduto buca la rappresentazione e de-stabilizza lo spazio (Schilder), das Ding chiama in causa una dimensione al di là della rappresentazione, dislocando temporaneamente ogni spazio rappresentabile e producendo un evento.

A partir del relato “La voz del bosque”, donde el narrador recuerda su primera lectura como un “acontecimiento” que dislocó su realidad, este artículo multidisciplinario muestra cómo la lectura crea espacios. En primer lugar, el análisis literario de este texto identifica tres tipos de configuración espacial entre la realidad y la ficción: la ubicuidad (Rosolato), los puntos de contacto (Freud) y la extimidad (Lacan). En segundo lugar, se examinan las numerosas pérdidas en el relato mediante un análisis metapsicológico de la lectura. Este análisis se basa en la relectura del texto de Freud sobre la “pizarra mágica” (Wunderblock) (la inexcitabilidad periódica del sistema perceptivo no solo contribuiría a formar representaciones de tiempo sino también representaciones de espacio) y en la puesta de relieve de las pulsiones en juego en la lectura, que dependen de una búsqueda de objetos perdidos (la mirada y la voz). La tercera parte del artículo conecta los tres tipos de configuración espacial con tres tipos de objeto presentes en el acto de la lectura: el objeto leído requiere un espacio geométrico representado y estable; el objeto perdido agujerea la representación y desestabiliza el espacio (Schilder); la Cosa (das Ding) participa de un más allá de la representación, dislocando temporalmente todo espacio representable y produciendo un acontecimiento.

responseSet.abstract:
[[RunningHead]]

Panel Report, IPA Congress Vancouver 2021 (Online): Falling into the Void: The Impact of COVID-19 on the Internal State of Children

Gretchen Schmutz

Gretchen Schmutz

Gretchen Schmutz  

Chair: Silvia Cantis Silberstein

Presenters: Reyna Cowan, Susana Ruth Merlo

Discussant: Judy K. Eekhoff

This engaging panel was introduced by Silvia Cantis Silberstein (Spain). Reyna Cowan (USA) and Susana Ruth Merlo (Argentina) presented papers with clinical material followed by a response paper by Judy K. Eekhoff (USA). These presenters are studying the impact of COVID-19 on the internal states of young children, and their ideas are sobering. Nonetheless, the clinical material imbues hopefulness by exemplifying how psychoanalytic work can improve the internal states of children trying to adapt to the reality of COVID-19. All three presenters underscore the pivotal role of the parent as a container for children’s anxieties and the impediments to containment presented by COVID-19.

Cowan argues that without sufficient containment by parents, intense annihilation anxieties emerge, including the fear of destruction of self and important others. She points out that the ordinary shifts from integrated to unintegrated states of mind seen in a child may become more frequent and severe. COVID-19 presents a serious challenge because a child can have dramatic increases in anxiety and have difficulty coping even with the help of parents. Given that COVID-19 is a collective experience, parents’ anxieties may get in the way of their capacity to be containing. Children may find alternative ways of coping including the concretization of fear around particular objects such as monsters, ghosts or spiders. Cowan gives other examples of the changed internal states of children including psychotic levels of anxiety, overproduction of ideas, precocity, and fears of aliveness versus deadness. Cowan’s clinical material helps us to see examples of the extraordinary anxiety she is trying to portray. She shows how primitive imagery of death and destruction can be consciously expressed rather than being disguised via derivatives.

Cowan’s paper also focuses on role of projective identification and posits: what happens to the child if the parent projects their beta-elements into their child? In this situation, the parent cannot be a container because the child is the receiver of the parents’ unmetabolized experiences. Cowan uses Bleger’s notion of symbiosis to elucidate the consequences for the internal state of the child when there is insufficient differentiation between the internal states of the parent and those of the child. This symbiosis may indicate that the child cannot separate from the parents and in this circumstance, the

responseSet.abstract:
[[RunningHead]]

Feminine law: Freud, free speech, and the voice of desire

Jill Gentile with Michael Macrone, New York, Routledge, 2018, 290 pp., $32.95 (paperback), ISBN-13: 978-1782202776

Sam Semper

Sam Semper

Sam Semper   samjsemper@gmail.com

Jill Gentile's ambitious and expansive work engages the two central questions of our time in the field of psychoanalysis. What is the relationship between clinical psychoanalysis and the political realm? – in this case, between psychoanalysis and democracy in the US. And how do we understand and undo the exclusion of the Other, here, the feminine Other, at the heart of both?

If some contemporary psychoanalytic discussions of gender risk abstraction, the loss of the material, the physical, as Rosemary Balsam warns, others risk conflating the material, the body, the morphology of the body, with gender and gender-inflected psychic experience. Gentile's call to explicitly name the vagina and the conflation of terms in her discussion begs the question of whether “woman” and “the feminine” begin to accrue meanings that are limiting or even dangerous. And whether democratic relations as she draws them out simply reify heterosexual complementarity and heterosexual union as the ultimate aim.

For Gentile, US democracy and psychoanalysis as discovered by Freud are intimately bound. And hope for ethical democratic relations, both interpersonally and in the polis, require the naming of the feminine, the vagina, which she argues is the founding exclusion in both. Gentile artfully connects the fundamental rule of free association in the practice of psychoanalysis and the foundational condition of free speech in a democracy, adding that democracy in the US and Freud's psychoanalysis were both conceived as a means of liberating people from repressive forces. Freedom in both exists in a “space between”, a metaphoric territory for open encounter. This third space is marked by limit on one side and absolute freedom on the other, conditions that make this space for encounter possible. The key to opening and ensuring this potential space is the naming of the vagina and what Gentile calls feminine law.

Gentile parallels the exclusion of “the woman” in Freud's psychoanalysis and US democracy, exclusions that have left a legacy of phallic monism that ignores female genital experience and a patriarchal meaning system built on the repressed invisible and unsymbolized feminine; this 421

responseSet.title: Credo? Religion and psychoanalysis: by Patrick Casement, London, Aeon, 2020, 72 pp, Book Price: £9.99, ISBN: 978-1912807635
responseSet.abstract:
[[RunningHead]]

Credo? Religion and psychoanalysis

by Patrick Casement, London, Aeon, 2020, 72 pp, Book Price: £9.99, ISBN: 978-1912807635

Edward Marriott

Edward Marriott

Edward Marriott   edmarriott@aol.com

Early in my first psychotherapy training, we were invited by the tutor to talk about why we had been drawn to the field. One of the group was a Catholic priest. I cannot now remember what he said. But I do recall the tutor’s response: “Well, you’re clearly trying to work something through.”

1059 It seemed as though the tutor felt that the priest, as a Christian, had more unresolved difficulties than the rest of us. In that moment, it felt that not much had moved on since Freud’s declaration in “The Future of an Illusion” that religion is “a universal obsessional neurosis” (Freud 1927, 43) and, as such, “ought to be given up, just as children relinquish their infantile neurosis as they progress in their development” (Quinodoz 2005, 227).

For Freud, the value of religious ideas lay in the fact that they helped defend man against the chaos that would ensure if all prohibitions against instinctual activity were relaxed. The idea of an omnipotent deity, furthermore, protected man against feeling helpless in the face of unpredictable and often cruel-seeming fate. As to the character of “God”, that depended on each individual’s relationship with his or her father: “Religion would thus be the universal obsessional neurosis of humanity; like the obsessional neurosis of children, it arose out of the Oedipus complex, out of the relation to the father” (Freud 1927, 43). In healthy development, then, just as the child gradually grows out of his infantile neurosis, so too should religious belief fall away.

“The Future of an Illusion” began a debate that continues to this day, with Freud’s friend and correspondent, the Lutheran pastor Oskar Pfister, being the first to object. While Freud saw no possibility for any reconciliation between psychoanalysis and religion, Pfister believed that psychoanalysis held out the possibility for believers to develop a more mature faith. Interestingly, Freud, recalled Ernest Jones, was

very fond of Pfister. He admired his high ethical standards, his unfailing altruism and his optimism concerning human nature. Probably it also amused him to be on unrestrainedly friendly terms with a Protestant clergyman, to whom he could address letters as “Dear Man of God” and on whose tolerance towards “an unrepentant heretic” – as he described himself – he could always count. (Jones 1953–57, II, 48)

responseSet.title: A commentary on Anzieu
responseSet.abstract:
[[RunningHead]]

A commentary on Anzieu

Marcelo Viñar

Marcelo Viñar

Marcelo Viñar   CONTACT Marcelo N. Viñar marvin@belvil.net The Uruguayan Psychoanalytical Association
Anzieu, French Psychoanalysis, Freud, Bleger, symbolic language

In a time and world in which the notions of transience and change have become key for analysing and understanding problems, commenting on a text conceived half a century ago and twelve thousand kilometres away from my home culture presents a challenge unlikely to meet with success. Yet paradoxically, thinking about the psychoanalysis of today in light of the past is an inevitable and necessary path for Freud’s successors. The controversy remains unsettled.

I was somewhat surprised reading this paper by Didier Anzieu. Instead of following the usual path of choosing a point to develop and exploring an issue in depth, Anzieu situates himself as a craftsman and takes several of our founder’s fundamental principles and concepts to affirm not only their purposes and advantages, but also the dangers and pitfalls to which they may give way. His focus is original and unusual.

His paper is divided into 16 paragraphs that structure the sequence. Right from the epigraph he adopts a provocative and challenging tone that, in response, creates more of a controversial reader than a devoted fan, and he demands a meticulous engagement with his agreements and discrepancies. He positions himself in the first person and demands of us the same. Freudianism refutes uniformity and obedience – this seems to be his central message.

Anzieu chooses four words of a noted poet for his epigraph: “Keep off the idolaters, bright watch-dog”. This epigraph strikes us in different ways before and after reading the text. Whom is it addressing? I take it as a warning or alarm bell about the concomitant advantages and dangers of the golden rule of our craft. The fragility of a subject in regression grants the subject’s interlocutor a power of suggestion that, in psychoanalysis, is only legitimate when not exercised. And the abyss of suggestion can be imminent.

Idolatry, in my view, consists of using masters as sacred leaders. This is a common vice among our tribe – one that suffocates the spontaneity of the Freudian experience, always marked by the surprise of the unexpected. The difference between a referential reading and a reverential one is never easy to establish. Anzieu warns of the risk of a biblical reading of Freud.

responseSet.title: The International Journal of Psychoanalysis Film Editor
responseSet.abstract:
[[RunningHead]]

The International Journal of Psychoanalysis Film Editor

The International Journal of Psychoanalysis is looking for a psychoanalyst to take up the honorary position of Film Editor, starting in July 2021.

The post holder will be knowledgeable about Film Studies and the interdisciplinary dialogue with psychoanalysis. The role will involve encouraging the submission and selection of essays to be published in the IJP, as well as some minor editing.

The Film Editor will have good written English, and knowledge of non-English languages is desirable.

Please send a short cover letter and CV to Catherine.humble@iopa.org.uk by March 31st, 2021.

responseSet.abstract:
[[RunningHead]]

Sigmund Freud et Romain Rolland: Un dialogue (Sigmund Freud and Romain Rolland: a dialogue)

by Henri Vermorel, Paris, Albin Michel, 2018, 640 pp., 29 Euros, ISBN-10: 2226435611. ISBN-13-978-2226435613

Nicolas Gougoulis

Nicolas Gougoulis

Paris

Nicolas Gougoulis   nico.gougoulis@wanadoo.fr

Light and darkness of an encounter: About Henri Vermorel's book

This recasting of a first edition (Vermorel & Vermorel, 1993) long out of print, is devoted to the relationship between Freud and Rolland. The new publication provides an opportunity to study this intellectual encounter, and pay tribute to the historical research of Henri Vermorel and Madeleine, his late wife. The first edition was part of the historical trend in France for studying Freud's work, as initiated by Alain de Mijolla. The first publications of the Vermorels are to be read in the context of the earliest publications of Freud's correspondence in France, and the second volume of the Revue Internationale d’Histoire de la psychanalyse, devoted to Freud's correspondence, presented it in this vein as an historical source, as evidenced by Gerhard Fichtner's pioneering research (1989). At that period, researchers studied almost all of Freud's correspondence for the first time. Vermorel, therefore, in his first edition, emphasized the turn-of-the-century spirit of the age with its profusion of intellectuals.

424

responseSet.title: The power of music: psychoanalytic explorations: by Roger Kennedy, Bicester, Phoenix House Publishing, 2020, 180 pp., £24.9, ISBN 978-1-912691-73-9
responseSet.abstract:
[[RunningHead]]

The power of music: psychoanalytic explorations

by Roger Kennedy, Bicester, Phoenix House Publishing, 2020, 180 pp., £24.9, ISBN 978-1-912691-73-9

Anthea Gomez

Anthea Gomez

Anthea Gomez   anthea.gomez1@gmail.com

Roger Kennedy’s book The Power of Music joins a small but growing library of psychoanalytic literature about what is arguably the most difficult of all subjects to articulate. It is a welcome addition and has a wide-ranging scope. The overarching question that preoccupies him is how to understand why music, western classical music in particular, affects the listener on such a deep, emotional and intellectual level. What is happening when we have a musical experience that puts us in touch with our feelings in this way and what are the origins of its power? “Music has the power to take us on an emotional and intellectual journey transforming the listener along the way; the aim of the book is to examine the nature of this journey” (1).

The prospect of this is appetizing and the reader is provided with a rich variety of ideas, thoughts and speculations derived from many fields and examined with scholarship and enthusiasm for the author’s subject. The subtitle of the book – Psychoanalytic explorations – gives an idea of Kennedy’s reference point as a psychoanalyst; however, he believes that to fully appreciate music’s impact on the emotions, one needs to piece together information from a variety of disciplines. He does this by not only looking at psychoanalytic connections and perspectives, but also bringing neuro-scientific, philosophical, cultural, evolutionary, biological and musicological views to the task. His method is thus to elucidate the question by examining it through a number of different lenses, as if approaching music as a multidimensional aesthetic object, while acknowledging that at its heart lies a mystery that “reaches into areas of experience where words leave off” (148).

The notion of a multiplicity of voices is one Kennedy previously elaborated in his book The Many Voices of Psychoanalysis (2007) and perhaps this gives a clue to his approach here. In that book, he writes that “the analyst needs access to a multiplicity of voices in order to take account of the complexity of the patient’s mind” (2007, 74). In his new book he writes:

no single discipline can do justice to music’s complexity, if one is to have a sense of the whole musical experience, even if one has to break up the whole experience into its various elements for the purposes of clarification. (2)

responseSet.title: The psychoanalysis-neurosciences interface: A proposal for a hypothetical unification of psychoanalytic models
responseSet.abstract:
[[RunningHead]]

The psychoanalysis-neurosciences interface: A proposal for a hypothetical unification of psychoanalytic models

Giampaolo Sasso

Giampaolo Sasso

Giampaolo Sasso   CONTACT Giampaolo Sasso giampaolo.sasso@fastwebnet.it Via Solari 11, 20144 Milano, Italy

Grotstein’s concept of projective transidentification led the author to reconsider the reasons that have led to the plurality of psychoanalytic models. The solution proposed is the existence of a fundamental frontal-occipital oscillatory dynamic, responsible for the projective-introjective dynamic that is at the basis of psychoanalytic theory and, at the same time, of the development and maintenance of mother–infant attunement. Such an oscillatory dynamic, according to this perspective, operates as a “bridge” between two seminal theoretical models of development – the psychoanalytic and the infant research model. A set of neurological hypotheses regarding how maternal interaction may act to modify the infant’s projective-introjective dynamic and general brain development is proposed. The different possible modifications of this dynamic offer an explanation of the variety and complexity of psychoanalytic models and the opportunity for a unitary approach, both clinical and theoretical. Given that it is considered to be the basis for cerebral activity, the oscillatory dynamic appears intrinsically connected with the default mode network’s functions of Self-cohesiveness and environmental monitoring, suggesting an important interface between psychoanalysis and the neurosciences.

Le concept de trans-identification projective de Grotstein a conduit l’auteur de cet article à reconsidérer les raisons qui ont abouti à une pluralité de modèles psychanalytiques. La solution proposée par l’auteur est celle de l’existence d’une dynamique frontale-occipitale oscillatoire fondamentale, responsable de la dynamique projective/introjective, qui est à la base de la théorie psychanalytique, et en même temps, du développement et du maintien de la syntonie mère/nourrisson. Une telle dynamique oscillatoire, selon cette perspective, fonctionne comme un « pont » entre deux modèles théoriques du développement fondamentaux – le modèle psychanalytique et le modèle des études mère-enfant. L’auteur propose une série d’hypothèses neurologiques sur la façon dont l’interaction maternelle peut agir et modifier chez le nourrisson la dynamique projective/introjective ainsi que, de manière générale, son développement cérébral. Les différentes modifications possibles de cette dynamique fournissent une explication sur la variété et complexité des modèles psychanalytiques et offrent l’opportunité d’une approche unitaire, à la fois clinique et théorique. Vu qu’elle est considérée comme étant la base de l’activité cérébrale, la dynamique oscillatoire apparaît comme étant intrinsèquement reliée aux fonctions du réseau du mode par défaut (DMN) de la cohésion du self et du monitoring environnemental, augurant d’une interface importante entre la Psychanalyse et les Neurosciences.

Rotsteins Konzept der projektiven Transidentifizierung veranlasste den Autor, die Gründe neu zu überdenken, die zu der Pluralität psychoanalytischer Modelle geführt haben. Der Lösungsvorschlag geht von der Existenz einer grundlegenden oszillierenden Dynamik zwischen frontalen und okzipitalen Arealen aus, die für die projektive und introjektive Dynamik verantwortlich ist, auf der die psychoanalytische Theorie ebenso basiert wie die gegenseitige Einstimmung von Mutter und Neugeborenem sowie die Beibehaltung dieser Einstimmung aufeinander. Diese Perspektive sieht eine solche oszillierende Dynamik als „Brücke“ zwischen zwei einflussreichen theoretischen Modellen der Entwicklung – des psychoanalytischen Modells und des Modells der Säuglings- und Kleinkindforschung. Es werden eine Reihe neurologischer Hypothesen dazu vorgestellt, wie die mütterliche Interaktion sich verändernd auf die Dynamik der projektiven und introjektiven Identifizierung des Säuglings bzw. des Kleinkindes und die allgemeine Entwicklung des Gehirns auswirken könnte. Die unterschiedlichen möglichen Modifikationen dieser Dynamik liefern eine Erklärung für die Vielfalt und Komplexität psychoanalytischer Modelle und bieten sowohl für die klinische Arbeit als für die Theorie die Gelegenheit, einen einheitlichen Ansatz zu verfolgen.

Da die oszillierende Dynamik als Grundlage für die Hirnaktivität gilt, scheint sie untrennbar mit den Funktionen Selbstkohäsion und Umweltkontrolle des Default Mode Network (DMN) verbunden zu sein, was das Vorhandensein einer wichtigen Schnittstelle zwischen der Psychoanalyse und den Neurowissenschaften nahelegt.

Prendendo le mosse dal concetto grotsteiniano di transidentificazione proiettiva, l’Autore riconsidera le ragioni che hanno portato alla creazione di modelli psicoanalitici diversi. Il motivo da lui individuato è che esiste una fondamentale dinamica oscillatoria frontale-occipitale a cui va ricondotta la dinamica proiettiva-introiettiva che sta alla base della teoria psicoanalitica e al tempo stesso anche dello sviluppo e del mantenimento della sintonizzazione tra madre e infante. Nella prospettiva qui delineata, la dinamica oscillatoria farebbe da “ponte” tra due dei principali modelli teorici dello sviluppo – quello psicoanalitico e quello dell’infant research. L’articolo propone anche una serie di ipotesi di ambito neurologico circa il modo in cui l’interazione materna può intervenire a modificare la dinamica proiettiva-introiettiva dell’infante e più in generale il suo sviluppo cerebrale. Le diverse configurazioni che questa dinamica può assumere aiutano a spiegare la varietà e la complessità dei modelli psicoanalitici, offrendo al contempo la possibilità di pensare un approccio unitario a livello sia clinico sia teorico. Poiché è considerata la base dell’attività cerebrale, la dinamica oscillatoria appare intrinsecamente connessa alle funzioni di default mode network (DMN) relative alla coesione del Sé e al monitoraggio dell’ambiente, prospettandosi pertanto come importante interfaccia tra psicoanalisi e neuroscienze.

A partir del concepto de transidentificación proyectiva de Grotstein, el autor revisa las razones que condujeron a la pluralidad de modelos psicoanalíticos. Propone como solución la existencia de una dinámica oscilatoria fronto-occipital fundamental, responsable de la dinámica proyectiva-introyectiva que se encuentra en la base de la teoría psicoanalítica y, también, del desarrollo y mantenimiento de la sintonía entre la madre y el bebé. Según esta perspectiva, la dinámica oscilatoria funciona como un “puente” entre dos modelos teóricos fundacionales del desarrollo: el psicoanalítico y el de la investigación infantil. Se plantea un conjunto de hipótesis neurológicas sobre la manera en que la interacción materna puede actuar para modificar la dinámica proyectiva-introyectiva y el desarrollo cerebral general del bebé. Las diferentes posibilidades de modificación de esta dinámica explican la variedad y la complejidad de los modelos psicoanalíticos y la oportunidad para un enfoque unitario, tanto clínico como teórico.

responseSet.title: Psychotherapy mediated by videogames
responseSet.abstract:
[[RunningHead]]

Psychotherapy mediated by videogames

Johann Jung and Guillaume Gillet

Guillaume Gillet

Bron
France

Lyon
France

Guillaume Gillet  

Our purpose in this article is to propose an overview of the specificities of therapeutic mediation through video games. We analyse the clinical implications of using a video game interface and its different functionalities. The use of the video game set-up involves requirements, like adjustment between the subject, the machine and the virtual environment. With the computer game interface, we are interested in the creation and use of an avatar. From a clinical case, we show how the avatar can be invested as an interface of communication with the other and with oneself. Another specificity of digital mediation by video games concerns what is called gameplay, that is to say, the set of internal rules of the software. The description of its different functions gives points of reference to how the processes of “symbolization”, according to Roussillon, can be mobilized in the video game worlds. Then we describe the specific forms of transfer in the use of the video game medium, based on the technical aspects of the medium and its immersive properties. The description of the different components of the video game set-up highlights the therapeutic potential of this type of medium.

Les auteurs de cet article dressent un tableau des spécificités de la médiation thérapeutique que constituent les jeux vidéo. Ils analysent les implications cliniques de l’utilisation d’une interface jeu vidéo et ses différentes fonctionnalités. L’utilisation de la configuration du jeu vidéo doit remplir certaines conditions, telles que l’ajustement entre le sujet, la machine et l’environnement virtuel. Ils s’intéressent à la création et à l’utilisation d’un avatar à partir de l’interface du logiciel de jeu vidéo. S’appuyant sur un cas clinique, ils décrivent la façon dont l’avatar peut être investi en tant qu’interface de communication avec l’autre et avec soi-même. le Gameplay, c’est-à-dire l’ensemble des règles internes du logiciel, fait partie des spécificités inhérentes à la médiation numérique à travers les jeux vidéo. La description de également ses différentes fonctions fournit aux auteurs un point d’appui pour montrer comment les processus de « symbolisation » – selon Roussillon – peuvent être mobilisés dans les univers des jeux vidéo. les auteurs décrivent ensuite les particularités des formes que revêt le transfert dans l’utilisation de la médiation par le jeu vidéo en mettant l’accent sur les aspects techniques de cette médiation et ses propriétés immersives. La description des différentes composantes du jeu vidéo met en lumière le potentiel thérapeutique de ce type de médiation.

Wir beabsichtigen mit diesem Beitrag, einen Überblick über die Besonderheiten der therapeutischen Behandlung mithilfe von Videospielen zu geben. Wir untersuchen die klinischen Auswirkungen des Einsatzes einer Videospiel-Benutzeroberfläche mit ihren unterschiedlichen Funktionsweisen. Für den Videospielaufbau müssen bestimmte Voraussetzungen geschaffen werden. Hierzu zählt beispielsweise die richtige Justierung des Subjekts, des Computers und der virtuellen Umgebung. Bei der Benutzeroberfläche des Computerspiels interessiert uns die Einrichtung und Verwendung eines Avatars. Anhand eines Beispiels aus der klinischen Praxis zeigen wir, wie der Avatar als Schnittstelle für die Kommunikation mit dem anderen und sich selbst eingesetzt werden kann. Eine weitere Besonderheit der digitalen Therapie mithilfe von Videospielen bezieht sich auf das so genannte Gameplay, also das interne Regelwerk der jeweiligen Software. Die Beschreibung ihrer verschiedenen Funktionen gibt Anhaltspunkte dazu, wie die Prozesse der von Roussillon definierten „Symbolisierung“ in den Videospielwelten mobilisiert werden können. Im Anschluss beschreiben wir die spezifischen Formen des Transfers bei der Nutzung des Mediums Videospiel, ausgehend von den technischen Aspekten des Mediums und dessen immersiven Eigenschaften. Die Beschreibung der verschiedenen Komponenten des Videospielaufbaus zeigen das therapeutische Potenzials dieses Mediums auf.

Il nostro intento nel presente articolo è di proporre una visione d’insieme degli aspetti che caratterizzano la mediazione terapeutica effettuata attraverso l’uso di videogiochi. Analizzeremo in primo luogo le implicazioni cliniche dell’uso di un’interfaccia videogame con le sue varie funzionalità. Ci sono alcuni requisiti necessari per poter utilizzare l’attrezzatura di un videogioco, come ad esempio l’adattamento tra soggetto, macchinario e ambiente virtuale. A interessarci soprattutto nell’uso dell’interfaccia del computer game sono la creazione e l’uso di un avatar. Attraverso una vignetta clinica mostreremo come si possa investire un avatar nella sua qualità di interfaccia comunicativa con l’altro e con se stessi. Un altro fattore specifico della mediazione digitale attraverso videogiochi riguarda il cosiddetto Gameplay – ossia, l’insieme di regole interne del software. La descrizione delle diverse funzioni del Gameplay rappresenta un punto di partenza per capire il modo in cui processi di “simbolizzazione”, secondo Roussillon, possano venire mobilitati nei mondi virtuali dei videogiochi. Passeremo poi a descrivere le specifiche forme del transfert in quel particolare contesto che è il videogioco, basandoci sugli aspetti tecnici del mezzo e sulle sue proprietà immersive. Una descrizione delle diverse componenti della configurazione del videogioco evidenzia infine il potenziale terapeutico di questo tipo di strumento.

Los autores proponen un panorama de las especificidades de la mediación terapéutica a través del videojuego. Analizan las implicancias clínicas del uso de dicha interfaz y sus diferentes funcionalidades. El uso de la estructura del videojuego implica requisitos como el ajuste entre el sujeto, la máquina y el entorno virtual. El interés por esta interfaz se centra en la creación y el uso del avatar. A partir de un caso clínico, se muestra cómo el avatar puede ser investido como una interfaz de comunicación con el otro y consigo mismo. Otra especificidad de la mediación digital a través del videojuego se relaciona con lo que se denomina gameplay, es decir, el conjunto de reglas internas del software o programa. La descripción de sus diferentes funciones brinda puntos de referencia acerca de cómo el mundo del videojuego puede movilizar procesos de simbolización, en el sentido de Roussillon. Se describen formas específicas de transferencia a través del uso del videojuego, basadas en los aspectos técnicos del medio y su capacidad de inmersión. La descripción de los distintos componentes de la estructura del videojuego pone de relieve el potencial terapéutico de este tipo de medio.

responseSet.title: A Discursive Study of the Reception of Lacanian Ideas and Their Relation to Kleinianism (Uruguay, 1955-1982)
responseSet.abstract:
[[RunningHead]]

A Discursive Study of the Reception of Lacanian Ideas and Their Relation to Kleinianism (Uruguay, 1955-1982)11

Gonzalo Grau-Pérez and Guillermo Milán

Guillermo Milán

Montevideo, Uruguay
gongrau@gmail.com

Montevideo, Uruguay

Guillermo Milán  

Kleinianism; Klein; Lacanianism; Lacan; Uruguay; discourse analysis

This research addresses the initial reception of Lacanian ideas in Uruguayan psychoanalysis. Lacanian ideas arrived in Uruguay in the 1960s in a context in which Kleinian thought prevailed. This paper studies the relation between Kleinianism and Lacanianism by analyzing discursive phenomena. The corpus used for this study was built from a variety of sources: theoretical-doctrinal papers, clinical cases, media publications, seminars transcripts, and institutional documents. The discursive materials presented here show the different controversies, crossroads, compromises, and demarcations that characterized the reception of Lacanian ideas in a context of Kleinian predominance.
